issues.title: Coverage: Users: list & search users
issues.body_raw:
**Domain:** Users
**Priority:** High
**Effort:** S (≤½ day)

## Background
Add coverage for the Users list/search endpoint and the GDPR personal-data
export. The wrapper currently has no method that calls `GET /almaws/v1/users`,
so consumers cannot list/search users without writing raw HTTP. This issue
fills that gap and exposes the personal-data accessor.

## Relevant Alma API documentation
- Alma dev-network: https://developers.exlibrisgroup.com/alma/apis/users/
- Endpoints touched:
  - `GET /almaws/v1/users`
  - `GET /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/personal-data`

Per the Alma docs, `GET /almaws/v1/users` accepts a richer optional query-param
set than the current proposal exposes — at minimum `limit`, `offset`, `q`,
`order_by`, `expand`, `source_user_id`, and `source_institution_code` are all
documented optional filters. The audit (2026-05-01) flagged the existing
signature as **narrower than docs** but not incorrect — adding the missing
optional filters is required before merge.

## Current wrapper behavior
No `list_users` / `search_users` / `get_user_personal_data` methods exist on
the `Users` domain class today. Callers fall back to the raw client. The
existing pattern reference is `BibliographicRecords.search_records`
(`src/almaapitk/domains/bibs.py:75`), which uses `q=` and `limit/offset`.

## Expected wrapper behavior

```python
def list_users(
    self,
    limit: int = 10,
    offset: int = 0,
    q: str = None,
    order_by: str = None,
    expand: str = None,
    source_user_id: str = None,
    source_institution_code: str = None,
) -> Dict[str, Any]: ...

def search_users(self, query: str, limit: int = 10) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    """Convenience wrapper over list_users(q=query) returning the user array."""

def get_user_personal_data(self, user_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]: ...
```

- `list_users` returns the full Alma envelope (`total_record_count`, `user[]`)
  so callers can paginate; `search_users` returns just the `user` array.
- `q=` follows Alma's specialized query syntax (e.g., `last_name~Smith`,
  `primary_id~12345`). Document a couple of examples in the docstring.
- `order_by`, `expand`, `source_user_id`, and `source_institution_code` are
  passed through to the request only when set (no Alma defaults injected).
- `get_user_personal_data` returns the full GDPR export payload as-is.

## Implementation notes
- Mirror `BibliographicRecords.search_records` for `q`/pagination handling.
- Validate required inputs (`user_id` non-empty for personal-data) and raise
  `AlmaValidationError`.
- Personal-data responses are sensitive — log only the user_id and result
  shape, never the body. Verify `alma_logging` redaction during testing.
- If `#11` (`iter_paged()`) lands first, prefer it for pagination over manual
  offset loops.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `list_users` exposes `limit`, `offset`, `q`, `order_by`, `expand`,
      `source_user_id`, `source_institution_code` and forwards them to the
      `GET /almaws/v1/users` query string only when provided.
- [ ] `search_users(query, limit)` is a thin wrapper over `list_users` and
      returns just the `user` array.
- [ ] `get_user_personal_data(user_id)` calls
      `GET /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}/personal-data` and returns the body.
- [ ] Type hints + Google-style docstrings on all methods.
- [ ] Unit tests (mocked HTTP) cover: pagination params, `q` syntax, missing
      `user_id` raises `AlmaValidationError`, personal-data redaction.
- [ ] At least one SANDBOX integration test for `list_users` and one for
      `get_user_personal_data`.
- [ ] No `print()` — all operations logged via `alma_logging`. Personal-data
      response bodies are NOT logged.
- [ ] `scripts/smoke_import.py` and `tests/test_public_api_contract.py` pass.
- [ ] If exposed publicly, added to `almaapitk/__init__.py` `__all__`.

## Open questions
- Should `search_users` keep its current return type (`List`) or switch to the
  full envelope for symmetry with `list_users`? Decide before merge.

## Audit notes (2026-05-01)
- Primary audit: **Partially aligned**. Endpoints correct; signature narrower
  than docs allow. Action: expose the additional optional query params
  (`order_by`, `expand`, `source_user_id`) or accept `**kwargs`.
- Codex direct audit: **Aligned** (didn't flag the missing optional params).
- Decision: take the stricter primary-audit recommendation; surface the
  documented optional filters explicitly rather than via `**kwargs` so
  IDE/type-check users get autocompletion.

## Files to touch
- `src/almaapitk/domains/users.py` (extend existing `Users` class)
- `tests/unit/domains/test_users.py`

## References
- Alma dev-network: https://developers.exlibrisgroup.com/alma/apis/users/
- Use the `alma-api-expert` skill for endpoint quirks and validation rules — query syntax is Alma's specialized format (e.g., `last_name~Smith`)
- Existing pattern: `BibliographicRecords.search_records` (`src/almaapitk/domains/bibs.py:75`)

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Recommended (would simplify or improve this work, but not strictly blocking):**
- #3 — Persistent requests.Session (every new HTTP call inherits connection pooling)
- #4 — Consolidate HTTP verbs into _request() (every new method routes through one chokepoint)
- #5 — Retry with exponential backoff for 429 / 5xx (every new method gets 429/5xx resilience for free)
- #9 — Map Alma error codes to specific exception subclasses (new methods raise typed Alma errors instead of generic AlmaAPIError)
- #10 — Propagate tracking_id and alma_code on errors (errors carry tracking_id for support escalation)
- #14 — Replace print() with logger; remove safe_request() (no print() statements in the new methods)
- #11 — iter_paged() generator at the client level (list/search methods should use iter_paged() instead of manual offset loops)

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Notes for the implementing agent
- Mirror an existing domain method as the pattern source (e.g., `Acquisitions.get_invoice` for read, `Acquisitions.create_invoice_simple` for write).
- All public methods must have type hints + Google-style docstrings.
- Validate required inputs at top of method and raise `AlmaValidationError` with a clear message.
- Log method entry with key parameters (info), success with result identifiers (info), and errors with full context (error).
- NEVER print — use `self.logger`.
- Test in SANDBOX first. Add at least one unit test under `tests/unit/` and one integration test under `tests/integration/`.
- After implementation, `poetry run python scripts/smoke_import.py` and the public-API contract test MUST pass.

- `list_users` returns a paged response with `total_record_count` and `user` array.
- `personal-data` endpoint is the GDPR data-portability export — used carefully.

---
Filed as part of the coverage-expansion backlog. See `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-30-coverage-expansion-design.md` for context.
